title: Issue 138 AI Advisory Profile Policy Run
subtitle: Governed run for the default advisory profile policy and document-native hierarchy work.
sourceCoverage: Issue #138, registry AI profile declarations, renderer AI-enhanced export code, AI-enhanced fixture, and e2e/contract tests.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when you need a first living doc for a feature and want to stay focused on the main surface.
templateMeta.operatingThesis: Start with the main product surface and its delivery tooling; grow only when design drift or verification pressure becomes structurally important.

objective: Resolve GitHub issue #138 by deciding and implementing the default advisory profile policy and document-native hierarchy for the first three AI export convergence types: design-code-spec-flow, verification-surface, and proof-ladder.
successCondition: Explicit registry/renderer contract for default aiProfiles, document-native advisory hierarchy, browser assertions for the first three convergence types, committed code, PR opened or updated, issue #138 updated, and verification evidence recorded.

callouts.title: Initial source constraint
items: The worktree is dirty before this run; stage only run-owned edits.
Do not mark objectiveReady until source changes are committed, verification runs, a PR path exists, and issue #138 is updated.

callout.title: Run status
items: Source commit b8b6696 implements the default advisory profile policy and hierarchy.
Draft PR #139 is open, issue #138 was updated, and the GitHub deliver check passed.

data.name: Default visible advisory policy
notes: The first three AI-enabled convergence types need a type-system-owned default visibility policy instead of relying on fixture-level section.ai.enabledProfiles as the implicit default.
Initial product stance: every enabled type gets a brief by default; focused weakness and next-loop profiles remain opt-in unless the section explicitly enables them.

data.name: Document-native advisory hierarchy
notes: The section brief is the reader-facing default orientation surface.
Weakest-signal and next-loop cards are secondary, opt-in, evidence-work surfaces that should appear below the brief when enabled.
Copy, labels, fallback text, and state messages should use section/advisory language rather than generic widget language.

data.name: Registry default policy contract
notes: Add explicit metadata to aiProfiles so default visibility is part of the registry contract.
Update registry contract tests to validate the new metadata and keep defaults constrained to declared profiles.

data.name: Renderer default-profile resolution
notes: Renderer currently only renders section.ai.enabledProfiles, so fixture declarations can masquerade as product defaults.
Change enabled profile resolution to include registry-declared default profiles, with section-level explicit profiles still enabling opt-in profiles.

data.name: Document-native advisory hierarchy
notes: Refine the section advisory heading, summary, card labels, fallback copy, success copy, and error copy so the export reads like a living-doc reader affordance.
Keep standalone read-only behavior intact and avoid expanding beyond the first three convergence types.

data.name: Draft PR delivery
notes: Draft PR #139 is open for the committed source changes.
GitHub check Living Doc Notify / deliver passed on PR #139.

data.name: Registry AI profile declarations
data.what_it_does: Declares aiProfiles for design-code-spec-flow, verification-surface, and proof-ladder.
why_it_matters: This is the correct source of truth for default advisory profile policy.

data.name: Renderer AI profile resolution and UI
data.what_it_does: Defines profile implementations, resolves enabled section tasks, renders the advisory section, styles it, and binds local-model runtime states.
why_it_matters: This is where registry policy must become reader-visible exported behavior.

data.name: AI-enhanced rendered-doc browser assertions
data.currentCoverage: E2e assertions now prove registry defaults render brief profiles without fixture-level explicit enabledProfiles, while opt-in weakness and next-loop profiles remain explicit.
gaps: Full npm test was not run because the worktree contains many pre-existing unrelated dirty changes outside this issue scope.

data.name: Registry and render contract tests
data.currentCoverage: Registry contract validates aiProfiles have ids, names, descriptions, supported slots, boolean defaultVisible metadata, and at least one default-visible profile per AI-enabled type.
data.nextStep: Keep renderer contract aligned with the section-advisory copy and embedded ai-render-graph spec boundary.
gaps: Full npm test was not run because the worktree contains many pre-existing unrelated dirty changes outside this issue scope.

data.name: Focused verification run
data.currentCoverage: Registry contract, render contract, first-three-type browser assertions, and GitHub Living Doc Notify / deliver passed after the policy and hierarchy changes.
gaps: Full npm test was not run because the worktree contains many pre-existing unrelated dirty changes outside this issue scope.

objectiveFacets.name: Frame objective
objectiveFacets.description: The objective and success condition are explicit enough to guide work.

objectiveFacets.name: Meet success condition
objectiveFacets.description: Explicit registry/renderer contract for default aiProfiles, document-native advisory hierarchy, browser assertions for the first three convergence types, committed code, PR opened or updated, issue #138 updated, and verification evidence recorded.

objectiveFacets.name: Hydrate sources
objectiveFacets.description: Relevant source material is discovered and linked into the document.

objectiveFacets.name: Implement or resolve
objectiveFacets.description: The core work is completed or a blocked conclusion is justified.

objectiveFacets.name: Verify outcome
objectiveFacets.description: Evidence supports the final status of the objective.

objectiveFacets.name: Finalize handoff
objectiveFacets.description: The final document records source links, residual risks, and completion state.

coverage.rationale: Issue #138 objective is represented as a policy decision with ticket linkage.
The success condition includes the selected hierarchy and default-reader policy.
Registry source anchor covers the type-system contract source.
Renderer source anchor covers exported behavior and local runtime binding source.
Registry metadata work is required for the explicit contract.
Renderer default-resolution work implements the reader-visible behavior.
Hierarchy refinement implements the document-native exported UX.
Browser assertions verify the default presentation and first-three-type behavior.
Contract tests verify the registry metadata schema and renderer integration assumptions.
Final handoff will record verification evidence, PR linkage, and issue update status here.
Focused verification evidence for the implemented policy and hierarchy changes.
PR and issue-update source artifacts satisfy final delivery handoff.

invariants.name: Status needs evidence
invariants.statement: Do not mark the objective complete unless verification evidence is linked to the relevant section or card.

invariants.name: Default policy owned by registry
invariants.statement: Default advisory visibility must be expressed in registry profile metadata and consumed by the renderer; fixture-only enabledProfiles must not be the canonical policy.

invariants.name: Document-native before widget
invariants.statement: Advisory UI hierarchy must read as a living-doc section affordance, not as a generic AI panel detached from section evidence.

invariants.name: Source detail owned by source
invariants.statement: Large, operational, canonical, or executable detail should live in a source artifact and be linked from the doc.
